phase9_code_challenges.title: Challenge 9.1: Build a simple Keras model
phase9_code_challenges.description: Create a tiny Keras Sequential model and print its summary (requires TensorFlow).
phase9_code_challenges.starter_code:
# Optional: requires TensorFlow
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

# TODO: build a tiny model and print summary
phase9_code_challenges.solution:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

model = Sequential([Dense(32, activation='relu', input_shape=(10,)), Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')])
model.summary()

phase9_code_challenges.title: Challenge 9.2: Train with Early Stopping
phase9_code_challenges.description: Build a Keras model and use an EarlyStopping callback during training on synthetic data.
phase9_code_challenges.starter_code:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping
import numpy as np

# TODO: build a model, compile it, and train with EarlyStopping
phase9_code_challenges.solution:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping
import numpy as np

X = np.random.rand(100, 10)
y = np.random.randint(0, 2, size=(100,))
model = Sequential([Dense(32, activation='relu', input_shape=(10,)), Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')])
model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
early = EarlyStopping(monitor='loss', patience=2, restore_best_weights=True)
model.fit(X, y, epochs=20, callbacks=[early], verbose=0)
print('Training complete')

phase9_code_challenges.title: Challenge 9.3: Use a Pretrained Feature Extractor
phase9_code_challenges.description: Load a pretrained TensorFlow model with include_top=False and build a new classifier on top of its output.
phase9_code_challenges.starter_code:
from tensorflow.keras.applications import MobileNetV2
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Flatten
from tensorflow.keras.models import Model

# TODO: load a pretrained model and extend it with a new output layer
phase9_code_challenges.solution:
from tensorflow.keras.applications import MobileNetV2
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Flatten
from tensorflow.keras.models import Model

base = MobileNetV2(weights='imagenet', include_top=False, input_shape=(64, 64, 3))
base.trainable = False
x = base.output
x = Flatten()(x)
x = Dense(64, activation='relu')(x)
predictions = Dense(10, activation='softmax')(x)
model = Model(inputs=base.input, outputs=predictions)
model.summary()
hints: Freeze base layers before adding new layers

phase9_code_challenges.title: Challenge 9.4: Build a Simple scikit-learn MLP
phase9_code_challenges.description: Train an `MLPClassifier` on synthetic data to bridge scikit-learn and deep learning concepts.
phase9_code_challenges.starter_code:
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier
import numpy as np

# TODO: create an MLPClassifier and fit it on synthetic data
phase9_code_challenges.solution:
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier
import numpy as np

X = np.random.rand(100, 10)
y = np.random.randint(0, 2, size=(100,))
model = MLPClassifier(hidden_layer_sizes=(50, 20), max_iter=200, random_state=42)
model.fit(X, y)
print(model.score(X, y))
hints: Fit on synthetic features and labels

phase9_code_challenges.title: Challenge 9.5: Build a Custom Keras Functional Model
phase9_code_challenges.description: Create a Keras model with the Functional API and print the model summary.
phase9_code_challenges.starter_code:
from tensorflow.keras import Input, Model
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

# TODO: build a functional model and print its summary
phase9_code_challenges.solution:
from tensorflow.keras import Input, Model
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

inputs = Input(shape=(10,))
x = Dense(64, activation='relu')(inputs)
x = Dense(32, activation='relu')(x)
outputs = Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')(x)
model = Model(inputs=inputs, outputs=outputs)
model.summary()
